definition: A node-edge structure with root branches paths and subtrees.
The starting or top node of a rooted tree.
A connection between parent and child.
A node immediately above another node.
A node immediately below another node.
A node and all of its descendants.
Distance from the root to a node.
Longest distance from a node to a leaf.
A tree in which each node has at most two children.
A binary tree preserving left-less-right-greater ordering.
A tree maintaining height constraints for predictable operations.
A tree representing strings or sequences through shared prefixes.
A tree representing nested program or expression structure.
A tree that routes cases through tests to outcomes.
A representation risk where tree structure hides overlapping or networked relationships.
